title: Saint John's Day
context:
I appreciate the detailed request, but I need to be direct about a significant limitation: my training data has a knowledge cutoff in April 2024, which means I cannot provide reliable specific information about events in September 2025. That date is approximately 17 months beyond what I can verify with confidence.

Here's what I can responsibly tell you:

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
September 14 in the church calendar is the Feast of the Holy Cross (Exaltation of the Holy Cross), observed annually. If this sermon title references "Saint John's Day," it may conflate this with Saint John the Baptist (June 24) or Saint John the Evangelist (December 27), which would be unusual for a September 14 date. The RCL readings for this date would focus on the triumph of the cross, likely Isaiah 45:21-25 and John 12:31-36. This falls in Ordinary Time, post-Pentecost.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS through INTERNATIONAL NEWS
I cannot reliably provide specific events for September 2025, as this exceeds my training data.

CULTURAL MOMENT
Same limitation applies—I cannot predict or report on the zeitgeist of September 2025.

What I'd recommend: Contact Saint John's Cathedral directly, reach out to the Episcopal Diocese of Colorado, and check local Denver news archives for that specific week to build an accurate snapshot. I can help analyze or contextualize information you gather from those sources.
